description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a slipper lobster, specifically resembling the Mediterranean slipper lobster (Scyllaridae family). Its body is robust and flattened, with a broad, shovel-like head shield that extends forward, giving it a distinctive appearance. The exoskeleton is mottled with shades of brown, reddish, and orange, featuring rough, granular textures and subtle banding along the segmented abdomen. The legs are short and sturdy, with several pairs visible beneath the body, and the tail is fan-shaped but not as pronounced as in other lobsters. The eyes are small and set close to the front of the head, while the antennae are short and flattened, blending into the head shield. The overall form is compact and armored, with no visible claws, making it distinct from true lobsters.
description: A robust, brownish slipper lobster with a broad, flattened head shield, short legs, and a segmented, armored body. Its surface is rough and mottled, lacking visible claws and featuring short, flattened antennae.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the slipper lobster, highlighting its broad head shield, segmented body, and multiple short legs.
description_view_1: Top-down view emphasizing the wide, flattened head shield and the granular texture of the exoskeleton, with the tail and body segmentation clearly visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the other profile of the lobster and the arrangement of its legs and tail.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the broad, shovel-like head shield, short antennae, and the compact, armored front of the body.
